result.thesis: POLARIS is an extraction-grounded fact conveyor whose admission predicate (provenance_generator.py:2930 `is_verified = len(failures) == 0`, with the NEUTRAL clause at entailment_judge.py:588 defining interpretation as failure) structurally forbids the sentence that earns insight — while the capabilities that would fix every one of its four weakest graded surfaces ALREADY EXIST IN THE TREE, dark by default: a purpose-built cross-source synthesis lane off behind three env flags (analyst_synthesis_deviation_check.py:155-203), a verified-numbers comparison-table renderer with no caller wired (presentation_tables.py:23-28), a research-report skeleton outline off behind two flags (multi_section_generator.py:947-1013), and venue metadata already sitting on every bibliography row (provenance_generator.py:4738) that RACE never sees because it only reads running prose. The push to SOTA is therefore not a rewrite: it is (a) lighting the dark lanes, (b) adding ONE genuinely new mechanism — a premise-grounded INFERENCE tier at the exact NEUTRAL branch (provenance_generator.py:2596-2621) that admits hedged interpretation whose every factual premise is span-verified, and (c) moving the report's judge-visible surface (paragraphing at :5064, venue names, sector organization, frame integration) to what the judge actually grades — because length and depth are measured dead, and every remaining point lives in synthesis, structure, and instruction compliance.
result.faithfulness_contract: TWO-TIER ADMISSION CONTRACT. TIER F (FACT) — unchanged, byte-identical when all new flags are off: a sentence is admitted iff it clears every existing leg of verify_sentence_provenance (provenance_generator.py:2098-2959) and `is_verified = len(failures) == 0` (:2930). TIER I (INFERENCE, body, PG_STRICT_VERIFY_INFERENCE_TIER): a sentence reaching the NEUTRAL branch (:2596-2621) is admitted iff ALL of: (1) it cites >=2 distinct evidence rows via provenance tokens — untokened sentences still die at :2186-2193, unchanged; (2) every MECHANICAL leg already passed — these run before entailment and are untouched, so every number, percent, integer, and binding qualifier in the sentence is already verified against the cited spans; (3) the first judge verdict was NEUTRAL — CONTRADICTED remains unconditionally fatal (:2607-2615 posture preserved); (4) the sentence carries an interpretive hedge cue (_INTERPRETIVE_CUE_RE, analyst_synthesis_deviation_check.py:219-226) — a bare categorical assertion is ineligible; (5) no named entity (author, study, organization, venue) appears in the sentence that is absent from the union of the cited rows' full text (reusing _span_grounds_sentence:138-152 and the author-attribution floor at :264-302); (6) a SECOND judge call with a premise-decomposition prompt returns INFERENCE_OK, defined as: every factual assertion in the SENTENCE is present in the SPAN, and the only content not in the SPAN is the hedged interpretive relation over those assertions — any other verdict, and any transport error, FAILS CLOSED to the existing entailment_failed drop (:2919-2923 posture preserved). Every Tier-I keep is tagged soft_warning='inference_tier' in the audit log and counted in telemetry, so the audit can enumerate exactly which sentences carry interpretation. TIER I-S (analyst section, existing dark-lane contract, unchanged from its authors' design): cited synthesis passes the deterministic anti-fabrication floor (every number in the union of cited sources' full text, >=2 content-word overlap); uncited interpretation passes _is_disclosable_interpretation (no decimals, no percent, no quantitative claim, no attribution absent from the pool, hedge cue mandatory); fabricated attributions and unhedged categoricals DROP; the section ships under an explicit interpretive disclosure. WHAT STILL GETS KILLED, everywhere, in every tier: any sentence contradicted by its spans; any unhedged interpretive assertion; any sentence introducing a number, percent, or named entity not groundable in the union of its cited sources; any untokened body sentence; anything on judge transport error. WHY NO HALLUCINATION HOLE OPENS: the only novel content class admitted anywhere is a HEDGED INTERPRETIVE RELATION whose operands are each independently span-verified — numeric and entity fabrication remain blocked by deterministic legs that run BEFORE and independently OF the judge; the change converts exactly one verdict path (NEUTRAL + hedged + premise-verified) from drop to labeled-keep, and every such keep is individually enumerable in the audit log for post-hoc inspection.
result.expected_gain: Honest arithmetic from 0.42-0.43 (treating Rank9/10/11 as one ~0.42 point with ±0.016 noise): midpoint sum of the eight changes is ~+7.6, but C1/C2/C4 overlap (all feed insight) and C6 is partly protective, so the defensible discounted estimate is +5 to +6.5 points -> a landing zone of 0.48-0.50. That RETAKES the published Gemini-2.5-Pro DR number (0.4888) and approaches human parity, but it does NOT reach 0.5265 — we fall an estimated 2.5 to 4.5 points short. The residual is not mechanical and I will not pretend a flag fixes it: (1) the QUALITY of interpretive prose — ADORE and Tavily beat the human reference, which means their synthesis reads BETTER than a human analyst's, and our hedge-mandatory contract deliberately caps assertiveness the judge may reward; (2) single-task measurement — every number here is task-72 with a ±0.016 floor, and the leaderboard averages 100 tasks, so per-task variance alone can eat 1-2 points of apparent gain; (3) the prior negative on insight directives says some fraction of C4/C8's estimate will not materialize. Path to closing the residual if the first wave lands at ~0.49: iterate the analyst-writer model and prompt (the one surface where prose quality is uncapped by the gate), and widen Tier-I eligibility gradually (3+ source inferences, comparative-magnitude claims) only as the audit log proves zero fabrications admitted.
result.kill_rule: A change is DEAD and reverts to default-off if ANY of: (1) its cheap mechanism test fails to prove the mechanism fired — no compose is ever purchased for a mechanism not yet proven live; (2) after two PAIRED composes (change-arm vs same-day control, identical config otherwise), its mean overall gain is <= +0.016 — the measured noise floor; one paired run is never sufficient because Rank9/Rank10 proved identical configs differ by 0.0158; (3) the audit log shows even ONE admitted sentence violating the faithfulness contract (a fabricated number, entity, or attribution, or an unhedged interpretive keep) — killed regardless of score, no appeal; (4) for insight-targeted changes specifically (C1/C2/C4/C8), the insight sub-score moves DOWN in both paired runs — the prior negative (0.4094 vs 0.4447) showed insight interventions can backfire, so insight changes are guilty until proven innocent; (5) body word count exceeds 9,500 judge-visible words (post-ArticleCleaner) — the length lever is measured dead and anything pushing past reference length is presumptively trading density for bulk. Changes are landed ONE ARM AT A TIME in weight order (C1+C6 together as a word-budget pair, then C3, C5, C7, C2, C4, C8); a surviving arm's flags join the control for the next arm, so every measurement stays paired against the best-known config.

result.reasoning:
REFUTED as proposed. Two independent hallucination holes, and they compound.

HOLE 1 — C5 itself ships an unverified fabrication surface (no Tier I needed). multi_section_generator.py:1883-1890: in facet mode the ONLY title validation is `if not title: continue`. The legacy allow-list check (`title_lower not in allowed`) is explicitly bypassed ("facet mode accepts ANY non-empty topical title"). Section/H3 titles carry no [#ev:] token and never enter verify_sentence_provenance. The >=2 evidence-ID floor (:1918-1926) only checks the IDs exist in the pool — never that they mention the sub-scope. So the scope addendum's "never fabricate a sub-scope the evidence cannot ground" is a PROMPT, not a GATE. An outline LLM asked to organize along "industries" when the pool has no sector-tagged rows will emit `### Manufacturing and Industrial Sectors` with any 2 ev_ids attached, and it ships — as an H3, the most prominent and least-verified position in the report. Their own admitted failure mode (a) says the pool may lack sector-tagged rows; they assume the outcome is a thin/gap-stub section, but the actual outcome is a FABRICATED HEADING, because nothing checks headings.

HOLE 2 — Tier I is blind to exactly the content class C5 creates.
- Leg 5 (named-entity floor) covers author/study/organization/venue. A SECTOR LABEL ("manufacturing", "financial services") is none of those. _span_grounds_sentence (:138-152) and the author-attribution floor (:264-302) cannot see it. The one novel content class C5 introduces is the one class no deterministic leg constrains.
- Leg 2 ("every MECHANICAL leg already passed, so every number is verified against the cited spans") equivocates PRESENCE with PREDICATION. provenance_generator.py:2200 states the numeric leg is a UNION check: "each decimal in the sentence is found in SOME cited span (union), not in every span." It verifies a number OCCURS somewhere in the union; it cannot verify WHAT the number is predicated of. Reattributing row A's clerical-task 27% to "manufacturing" is invisible.
- Leg 1 (>=2 distinct rows), sold as a safety requirement, is ANTI-safety: it strictly widens the numeric union and widens `combined_span = " ".join(aggregated_span_text)` (:2586) that the judge sees. A 2-token sentence is mechanically WEAKER than a 1-token one. Requiring >=2 rows increases the attack surface it claims to shrink.
- Leg 3 (NEUTRAL-only): NEUTRAL is precisely the verdict a judge returns when the SPAN IS SILENT about the claim — which is exactly the verdict a fabricated sector attribution produces. Tier I converts "the span says nothing about this" from DROP to keep-if-judge-2-agrees. That is the definition of admitting ungrounded content.
- Leg 4 (hedge cue) is porous: _INTERPRETIVE_CUE_RE (analyst_synthesis_deviation_check.py:219-226) matches `represent\w*`, `remain\w*`, `requir\w*`, `poses?`, `reflect\w*`, `align\w*`, `dimension\w*`. "Manufacturing REMAINS the most exposed sector" is a bare categorical that clears the "hedge" gate. Hedging is a lexical proxy, not grounding — and a hedged false attribution is read by graders and users as fact.

CONCRETE PATH: pool has row A (clerical automation 27%) and row B (economy-wide exposure 14%); neither mentions manufacturing. C5 emits `### Manufacturing and Industrial Sectors` [A,B] (accepted unconditionally). Composer writes under the heading: "Manufacturing remains the most exposed sector, with 27% of tasks automatable, while broader exposure sits nearer 14% [#ev:A][#ev:B]." 2 rows ✓; both decimals in the union ✓; no author/org entity ✓; judge-1 = NEUTRAL (span silent) ✓; hedge cue "remains" ✓. Every deterministic leg has waved it through and a SINGLE LLM judge call is the only thing between a false sector attribution and the report. That is not a moat.

COMPOUNDING: Tier F would render a sector-thin section as _GAP_STUB_SENTENCE (:1044) — honest. Tier I is what lets it render as confident, hedged, numerically-decorated prose. C5 GENERATES demand for exactly the sentence class Tier I newly admits. They are not independent changes.

BUNDLING: Tier I is not required by C5 at all. Shipping them together means a faithfulness regression cannot be attributed or unwound, and any score win cannot be attributed to structure vs. to newly-admitted previously-dropped unverified sentences. That alone refutes the package as stated (and compounds their own admitted confound (b)).
result.fix:
Split the package and add the missing deterministic gate.

1. SHIP C5 WITH TIER F ONLY. Drop Tier I from this change entirely; evaluate it as its own arm against an adversarial mis-attribution probe set (true operands, false relation), never first on a section type whose failure mode IS mis-attribution.

2. CLOSE THE HEADING HOLE (required even for Tier-F-only C5). Add a deterministic sub-scope grounding gate in _parse_outline's facet branch (multi_section_generator.py:~1883), since facet mode removes the allow-list and currently accepts any non-empty title: for each sub-scope H3 under the scope section, require the title's content words (or a synonym set derived FROM THE EVIDENCE TEXT, never from the model) to appear in the FULL TEXT of >= 2 of its assigned rows. Sub-scopes that fail are dropped. If fewer than 2 sub-scopes survive, drop the whole scope section rather than shipping a 1-sector "sectoral coverage" body. This makes "never fabricate a sub-scope" enforceable instead of aspirational.

3. IF TIER I IS EVER REVISITED: it needs a deterministic leg that constrains the RELATION, not just the operands — e.g. every subject-of-predication noun phrase in the sentence must appear in the SAME cited row that supplies the number bound to it (per-token slice, not the union at :2200). Until that exists, a NEUTRAL verdict on a span that is merely SILENT is indistinguishable from a NEUTRAL on a span that is merely IMPRECISE, and only the latter is safe to keep.

result.reasoning:
REFUTED — but not for the reason the lens suggests, and I want to be precise about which half of the attack lands.

WHAT SURVIVES (be honest): this is NOT the STEP-3 insight directive with new words. STEP-3 was a *prompt directive* to the writer to produce insight (A/B negative: 0.4094 vs 0.4447 control). This is a deterministic, post-verify, content-neutral render pass — no new claims, no writer-prompt change. And unlike EVERY lever refuted since Rank7 (length, padding removal, source-eligibility firewall), it is *not* invisible to the scorer: `ArticleCleaner` strips citations/bibliography, but the reference's post-cleaner profile (24 H3 / 10 tables / 91 bullets, FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md:140) proves markdown structure SURVIVES the cleaner. So the "invisible to the judge" refutation, which killed Rank12, does NOT transfer. The mechanical diagnosis at /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/src/polaris_graph/generator/provenance_generator.py:5064 is correct and I verified it: one `" ".join(findings_lines)` per section is literally why we have 12 paragraphs.

WHY IT IS STILL REFUTED — four independent hits, any one of which is fatal to the CLAIM as written (+0.8 to +1.4):

1. THE CLAIMED EFFECT IS AT OR BELOW THE NOISE FLOOR, AND THE ARM AS PITCHED CANNOT SEE IT. Claimed +0.008 to +0.014 overall vs. the measured n=1 noise floor of 0.0158 (Rank9 vs Rank10, byte-identical config: 0.4155 vs 0.4313). Even the TOP of the claimed range is inside one noise width. The log's own standing rule and its own NEXT STEP already say this: "EVERY lever in both plans is SMALLER THAN OUR MEASUREMENT NOISE... score a byte-identical report k=5 to pin the true per-call SD, then ONE bundled arm scored 5x paired (SE ~0.008)." Shipping this as an n=1 arm reproduces exactly the error that made the driver "confidently wrong four times running."

2. THE READABILITY ARITHMETIC IS A FABRICATION OF THE TARGET. "0.3774 -> ~0.45 alone is +1.0" back-solves the needed gain from the desired conclusion. 0.45 readability has NEVER been observed on this task: our best-ever readability is 0.4008 (CTRL, 3.3k words, which has the SAME 0-H3/0-table/0-bullet profile as every other arm). Across six scored arms with IDENTICAL structural profiles, readability ranged 0.3379 -> 0.4008 — a 0.063 spread with ZERO structural difference, and the noise-floor replicate alone moved readability 0.3588 -> 0.3774. The dimension's observed variance nearly equals the entire claimed gain, and the arm that scored BEST on readability was the SHORTEST one, not the most structured one. There is no evidence in our own data that structure markers are what this judge is grading.

3. THE TASK-72 READABILITY RUBRIC DOES NOT PAY FOR WHITESPACE — AND THE PROPOSED MECHANISM ATTACKS TWO OF ITS SUB-CRITERIA. Decomposing race_task72_criteria.txt: L1 language/tone (0.20) unchanged (sentences verbatim); A1 (0.10) unchanged; S1 macro-structure (0.20) is about a scope-setting intro, an organizational map, and a synthesizing conclusion — H3 labels on ev-adjacency groups touch it only marginally. The only lines that plainly pay are F1 formatting/layout (0.10 — and half of F1 is about in-text citation and bibliography formatting, which the cleaner DELETES for both reports) and D1 (0.10, and only "if the review itself uses tables"). REFUTED. C6 does open a faithfulness hole — and it does not need Tier-I to do it. Three of the four gates are unsound as specified, and the padding premise behind the fourth is factually false against the code.

=== 1. THE HALLUCINATION PATH: post-verify byte mutation (gates 1 + 4)

POLARIS's moat is a per-sentence contract: `is_verified = len(failures) == 0` (/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/src/polaris_graph/generator/provenance_generator.py:2930) binds ONE assertion string to ITS cited spans. The render loop then emits `sentence_out = stripped + markers` (:5045). Everything downstream of :2930 is unverifiable by construction — verify already ran and cannot re-run.

PG_RENDER_HYGIENE inserts NEW string surgery after that line. It explicitly proposes to "strip fact_dedup's internal cross-reference sentences ('...is detailed under <section>') from rendered prose." Look at what fact_dedup actually produces (fact_dedup.py:845-860, REWRITE_SYSTEM_PROMPT): the cross-reference IS the whole sentence, rule 1 requires it to CARRY an `[ev_XXX]` marker, and rule 4 requires it to PRESERVE the topical anchor — "Regressivity by income is detailed under {PRIMARY_SECTION} [ev_X]". That entire string is what strict_verify graded and what `verifier_pass` attests. Any implementation that strips the "...is detailed under <section>" CLAUSE rather than dropping the whole sentence ships a byte-sequence that was NEVER verified, still carrying its `[N]` marker — i.e., a citation asserting that a span supports a claim the span was never checked against. That is literally an ungrounded claim reaching the report, with a citation on it. Same hazard in "fix the mid-word section starts": the existence of mid-word starts is proof that some pass ALREADY corrupts verified prose post-render. The response to a renderer known to mangle verified bytes is not to add three more mutation passes to it.

The only safe form of these gates is whole-sentence DROP, never clause-edit — and the design does not say that.

=== 2. THE SECOND HALLUCINATION PATH: deletion mutates the truth conditions of surviving verified sentences

verify_sentence_provenance (:2098-2959) has NO cross-sentence leg. Every check is sentence-local against that sentence's own spans. Document meaning is not compositional that way. Two concrete failures:

(a) Antecedent stripping. fact_dedup's cross-ref sentence exists precisely to be the topical anchor for the section's remaining prose (its own prompt: "readers still see what the cross-ref points to"). Strip it, and the next sentence — "This regressivity is most acute in the bottom quintile [ev_9]" — re-binds "this" to whatever fact now precedes it. The shipped report asserts a relation the evidence does not support, assembled entirely from individually-verified bytes. Nothing in the pipeline can catch this.

(b) Qualifier stripping. PG_DROP_METADATA_SENTENCES targets "venue/volume/citation-count metadata with no outcome claim." That description also matches the evidence-strength caveat — "The only source reporting this is a non-peer-reviewed preprint [ev_07]", "this appears in a single 2019 conference abstract [ev_12]": venue metadata, zero outcome numbers, high semantic load. Delete it and the neighboring claim silently promotes from hedged to categorical. That is exactly the failure the whole overstatement_guard.py module exists to prevent, and the clinical writer prompt explicitly mandates ("Hedging: adjust claim strength to evidence strength", multi_section_generator.py:~3735). The regex cannot distinguish "received 239 Scopus citations" from "this is the only source and it is a preprint" — both are venue/metadata with no outcome number. Their admitted failure mode (a) understates this: it is not only C7-interaction, it is deletion of the pipeline's own epistemic hedges.

=== 3. GATE (3) IS FACTUALLY MISTARGETED AND IS FAITHFULNESS-NEGATIVE

The premise "the unsatisfiable rule #9 makes the writer pad by restating" is false. Rule #9 (multi_section_generator.py:3760) reads: "cite at least 5 DISTINCT sources across this section." Sections receive up to PG_MAX_EV_PER_SECTION=30 evidence rows (multi_section_generator.py:2664). A 5-distinct-source floor against a 30-row slice has 6x headroom — it is not unsatisfiable, so it cannot be the padding driver.

The actual padding drivers, all left untouched by the proposal:
- rule #8: "Target 10-18 sentences... do NOT stop short"
- rule #9's SECOND clause: "Every named entity, every numeric estimate, every specific finding should be its own cited sentence" (forced atomization -> verbosity)
- rule #10: "Synthesize converging sources into each sentence to raise citation density"

So gate (3) deletes the one clause that is NOT causing padding — the distinct-source DIVERSITY floor, the only rule preventing single-source over-reliance — and keeps every clause that is. Result: citation diversity collapses (their own admitted failure mode b), padding persists, zero words saved, and single-source assertion rate rises, which is precisely what drives DeepTRACE unsupported/one-sided rates the wrong way. It also starves the multi-citation basket render machinery (I-arch-005 B6/B8, provenance_generator.py:~4990), which only has anything to show when claims are genuinely multi-source.

=== 4. THE AUDIT BUNDLE STOPS DESCRIBING THE SHIPPED REPORT — BY DESIGN

The proposal's own safety story is the tell: "drops from RENDER only, kept in audit log so faithfulness telemetry is unchanged." That is a deliberate decoupling of the audit from the artifact.
- benchmark/dimension_scorers.py:176 scores `s for s in section.verified_sentences if s.verifier_pass` — so the faithfulness score is computed over sentences that DID NOT SHIP.
- audit_bundle/manifest_builder.py:99 and audit_bundle/snapshot_sources.py:56 build the CUSTOMER-FACING provenance manifest from that same list — so the audit bundle would attest spans for sentences absent from the report.
- It re-introduces exactly the overstatement that the F10 comment at provenance_generator.py:5054-5058 was written to kill: "the count of sentences ACTUALLY emitted... NOT len(kept_sentences), which overstates."

For a product whose entire moat is "you can audit every sentence," shipping an audit bundle that describes a different document than the PDF is not a shippable state. "Telemetry is unchanged" is not a safety property here; it is the metric being pinned while the artifact moves underneath it.

=== 5. THE VALUE CLAIM DOES NOT STAND ALONE

C6 and C7 are antagonistic: C7 ADDS venue attribution, C6's regex DELETES venue metadata. "Order the passes: venue attribution runs on SURVIVORS" sequences them but does not resolve that one change's product is the other's definition of padding — either C7's attributions survive (C6 saves nothing) or C6 eats them (C7 is a no-op). And C6's stated main value is "protective — it buys back budget for C1." If C1 does not land, C6 is a pure word-count reduction with no refill: that is Rank8's regression, which they admit as failure mode (c), in a repo whose own history (commits fb228dd, e87f52e) shows stacked levers breaking the length/density trade-off and floors adding zero.

Gate (2) (omit the empty contradiction stub) is the only defensible piece, and even it is grader-optimization: deleting a TRUE negative-result disclosure because a judge dislikes it makes the report less honest about its own epistemic state (the reader can no longer distinguish "checked, found none" from "never checked"). Gates (1), (3), (4) as specified are net-negative on faithfulness. Refuted.

Tier I converts `NEUTRAL + hedged + premise-verified` from drop to keep. NEUTRAL is precisely what you get when the span is *silent* about a proposition — not when it refutes it. CONTRADICTED requires the span to actively say otherwise. A span about clerical task-exposure percentages can never *contradict* "this reflects AI as a 4IR driver"; it is simply mute on it. - **Tier F only (gate intact):** the bolt-on frame sentences NEUTRAL-drop. You recover the ~4 name-drops you already have — i.e., the exact "name-drop padding" defect C8 was written to fix — plus the prior-negative risk: the last "insight directive" A/B **lost 3.5 points**, and C8 adds *more* soft directive text to the same section prompt that already carries 10 hard evidence-only rules. Expected value ≤ 0.
- **Tier I on (gate relaxed):** the sentences survive, the INTEGRATION metric goes up, the graded criteria are satisfied — **and the mechanism producing the score is fabrication.** The cheap test ("frame terms co-occurring with findings in the same paragraph") is *maximized* by the ungrounded hedged bolt-on and cannot distinguish it from real integration. The eval gradient points directly at the hole and will report it as success.

C8 is either safe-and-worthless or valuable-and-unfaithful. There is no configuration in which it is both safe and worth the two graded criteria.

The reference report opens with 4IR because **its evidence base contains 4IR-framing sources**. POLARIS's pool has 4 mentions. That is a *retrieval* deficit, not a *prompting* deficit. No prompt directive and no gate relaxation can make the corpus say something it does not say — it can only make the writer say it without the corpus.

BONUS: the companion firewall doesn't even target the violating class. Excluding "T5/T6/profile-page" rows removes 2 T6 + 2 T7 = ~4 of 107 rows (~4%). Every actual criterion violation sits in T1-T4: PubMed Central at T1, arXiv/SSRN at T4, OECD/IMF/ILO/BLS/CRS institutional reports at T3 (38 rows). Widening it to catch those would delete ~half the corpus and collapse the word count — the known score killer.

result.reasoning:
REFUTED on four independent grounds, the first of which is dispositive.

1) THE PRIOR NEGATIVE IS EXACT, NOT ANALOGOUS. C6 is padding removal. Padding removal has already been run and scored: Rank11 (cross-section repetition guard) deleted ~2,000 words of genuine padding (9,194 -> 7,178 words) and OVERALL went 0.4313 -> 0.4133; readability 0.3774 -> 0.3802 (+0.003, inside noise). FLYWHEEL_PROGRESS.md records it verbatim: "The padding was real and it was worth nothing -- but removing it was also worth nothing. Clean cheap negative." And: "length -> no movement; padding removal -> no movement; source compliance -> no movement. EVERY lever pulled since Rank7 has been INVISIBLE TO THE SCORER." C6 removes 800-1,500 words -- LESS THAN HALF of what Rank11 already removed for zero effect. It is not a new idea with new words; it is the same idea at smaller magnitude.

2) THE PADDING C6 NAMES DOES NOT EXIST IN THE SHIPPED TEXT. I scanned the bodies of /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/outputs/{rank7,rank9,rank10,rank12}_*/report.md. Citation-count metadata sentences (regex 'received N citations|Scopus citations|has been cited|citation count'): 0 hits in rank9, rank10 and rank12 (the current best config); exactly 1 hit in rank7 -- and it is the single instance the proposal quotes ("received 239 Scopus citations"). fact_dedup cross-reference sentences ("...is detailed under <section>"): 0 hits. The contradiction stub is one ~25-word clause inside the Limitations line. Total addressable text: ~25-50 words of 6,379 body words (<1%), not the asserted 30-35%. That figure is asserted, never measured. So PG_DROP_METADATA_SENTENCES has an expected yield near zero while its admitted failure mode (a) is live: a broad metadata scan of rank12 returns 5 sentences and 4 are substantive content ("One systematic review guided by PRISMA 2020 searched Scopus and Web of Science to include studies...", the worker-inclusion finding). Its false-positive surface strictly exceeds its true-positive surface.

3) THE PROTECTIVE PREMISE ("Rank7 showed length becomes strictly harmful past reference length") IS FALSE ON THE SCORECARD. Rank10 9,194w = 0.4313 (HIGHEST arm); Rank9 7,937w = 0.4155; Rank7 7,615w = 0.4041; Rank8 4,226w = 0.3957 (LOWEST). Score is if anything weakly POSITIVELY associated with length; Rank7's score cannot be blamed on length since Rank9/10 are longer and score higher. And the headroom C6 buys back is not scarce: post-cleaner the 0.5000 reference is ~59 paragraphs x 142 words ~= 8.4k words while our post-cleaner text is 7.7k -- we are BELOW reference length. Worse, the reserved budget is smaller than the noise in the thing reserved: identical config, two runs gave 8,055 vs 9,300 words (+/-15% report, +/-22% body). You cannot bank 800-1,500 words in a system whose word count swings +/-1,245 at fixed config; the standing rule in the log is "treat any word-count or density gap under ~20% as NOISE".

4) THE CLAIMED GAIN IS UNDER HALF A NOISE WIDTH AND THE RUBRIC CAPS IT THERE. +0.3 to +0.8 points = +0.003-0.008 on the 0-1 scale against the measured +/-0.016 n=1 floor (Rank9/Rank10 are the SAME config and differ by 0.0158). Detecting the top of that range needs ~k=10 paired runs; the bottom ~k=40. Analytical ceiling agrees: redundancy/informativeness is graded under readability P1 (w=0.15 within readability's 0.14 = 0.021 of overall), with L1/D1/F1 adding at most as much again; a perfect +0.2 absolute move on those criteria yields ~+0.004-0.008 overall. The proposal's own optimistic ceiling sits at half the noise floor -- it cannot be seen even if it is real. The insight leg ("removes the anti-insight stub and the density dilution") is weaker still: it rests on the density thesis, and density was already withdrawn as within-noise (+1.32 against a 1.70 floor), while Rank12 showed a lever aimed squarely at a dimension failed to move that dimension.

5) IT CARRIES REAL DOWNSIDE ON THE ONE STABLE METRIC. PG_SECTION_RULE9_SOFT rewrites rule #9 (multi_section_generator.py:3760) in the writer prompt of the ONLY config with scored baselines (clean pool + 28-36 + cap30 + tier-first + distinct=15). Distinct works cited is one of only two metrics the variance probe found STABLE and it feeds comprehensiveness ("Depth and Representativeness of Literature Synthesized", w=0.15 of 0.29). C6 trades a stable, load-bearing metric for an unmeasurable readability gain, and run alongside C1 it confounds the one arm worth running.

WHAT SURVIVES: one bug, not a lever. 2 of 11 sections in rank12 ship TRUNCATED FIRST WORDS -- "## Employment and Displacement Effects" opens "ceptible to potential artificial intelligence impacts, 2023-33 |..." and "## Conclusions and Research Gaps" opens "d and the specific skill sets involved.[82]". That is genuine judge-visible prose corruption (readability L1). The empty-contradiction stub also survives ArticleCleaner and should go. Neither justifies an env flag, a new gate, or a scored arm against a +/-0.016 floor.
result.fix: Do NOT ship C6 as an arm. Keep ONLY the two byte-level defect fixes, unflagged, folded into C1's arm as ordinary cleanup with no separate scored run and no claimed score delta: (a) the mid-word section starts on the render path (/home/polaris/wt/flywheel/src/polaris_graph/generator/provenance_generator.py:5030-5065) -- rank12 ships 2/11 sections whose opening word is chopped ("ceptible to potential..." and "d and the specific skill sets involved.[82]"); (b) suppress the empty-contradiction stub at /home/polaris/wt/flywheel/src/polaris_graph/retrieval/contradiction_detector.py:2477 so the detector emits nothing instead of "No contradictions detected in the evidence corpus." result.thesis: C1 and C2 are DEAD and stay dead — no rebuttal survives contact with the code. I verified the C1 kill shot myself: the scored composer's report assembly (scripts/compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:652-668) renders only sr.verified_text + Limitations + References; analyst_synthesis_text never reaches report.md, so the env-only flip is mechanically score-invisible while arming ~100 discarded LLM calls and corrupting total_words. C2's NEUTRAL-keep converts 'span does not support this claim' into a keep with only numeric deterministic backstops, which is the hallucination hole our own pre-registration declared unshippable. The thesis that DOES survive — endorsed independently by all three skeptics as the 'cheaper alternative' — is the pre-registered bundled arm: rebuild the DOCUMENT (H3s, 150-word topic-sentence paragraphs, study tables, bullets) and weave interpretation OVER frozen verified contrasts, at zero added words, implemented as an offline rewrite stage on the banked Rank10 artifact so the whole wave needs ZERO compose runs and is scored 5x-paired against a k=5-pinned baseline. Faithfulness moves from 'one tier with a soft verdict' (refuted) to 'disjoint sentence classes with deterministic membership': facts never bypass the frozen verifier, interpretation is deterministically barred from carrying any checkable factual atom, and everything else fail-closes.
result.faithfulness_contract: Every sentence in the shipped report belongs to exactly ONE class, decided by deterministic checks that run before and independent of any LLM, fail-closed at paragraph granularity (any unclassifiable sentence reverts its whole paragraph to the source text). CLASS F (FACT): either byte-identical to an already-verified sentence (the reflow validator asserts the multiset of citation-bearing sentences is preserved), or a rewritten/fused sentence that re-passes the FULL frozen verify_sentence_provenance gate (provenance_generator.py:2098 — all deterministic numeric legs + entailment) against the union of its parent sentences' cited rows (spans from bibliography.json direct_quotes + corpus); NEUTRAL or any verifier failure = revert to the parent sentences, never keep, never drop content. Only class F may carry [n] citations or numbers. CLASS S (STRUCTURE): headers, transitions, and table cells assembled deterministically from sidecar metadata and verified-sentence substrings — zero LLM-originated factual content by construction. CLASS I (INTERPRETATION — the only new admitted content) is legal iff ALL of: (1) contains no digits, percents, units, or comparative magnitudes; (2) every proper noun / capitalized multi-token span already appears verbatim in the same paragraph's class-F sentences or the task-prompt concept whitelist — this kills fabricated attribution including the single-name/org gap the skeptics found in _AUTHOR_ATTR_RE; (3) carries no citation marker, so it can never masquerade as verified; (4) is adjacent to ≥2 class-F sentences citing ≥2 distinct sources and shares ≥2 content lemmas with them — it must interpret the compared findings, not free-float; (5) uses an epistemic frame and no universal/predictive-certainty pattern; (6) passes a MANDATORY contradiction screen — the existing sentinel judge with the paragraph's class-F sentences as premise, CONTRADICTED = dropped, judge error/timeout = dropped. This is kill-on-contradiction, NOT the refuted keep-on-NEUTRAL: because class I is deterministically barred from carrying any checkable factual atom, NEUTRAL here means 'a hedged relational gloss the premises neither entail nor deny', not 'an ungrounded fact'; (7) volume caps: ≤1 per paragraph, ≤8% of body sentences, and EVERY kept class-I sentence is enumerated verbatim with its premises in the audit sidecar (closing the count-only audit gap the skeptics documented) and manually read before any score is trusted. WHAT STILL GETS KILLED, unconditionally: any new number outside the frozen verifier; any new named actor; any [n] on unverified text; anything a judge calls contradicted; everything when the judge is unavailable; any LLM paraphrase of a class-F sentence that fails re-verification. GUARANTEE: the only wrong-but-shipped content possible is an explicitly hedged, uncited, volume-capped, contradiction-screened relational gloss over correct verified facts — no fabricated fact, number, quote, or attribution can ship, because those are excluded by deterministic string-level checks, not by model judgment. The skeptics' four concrete attacks (polarity inversion of the 14% finding, Goldman-Sachs attribution, cross-source number transplant, 'replace all lawyers') are encoded as permanent regression tests that must all DROP before any judge call is spent. The report preamble's verification claim (compose_agentic_report_s3gear329.py:644-651) is amended, not softened: it additionally discloses that connective interpretive sentences carry no citations and no quantitative claims.
result.expected_gain: Honest accounting from the TRUE baseline ~0.42 (Rank9/10/11 mean; quoting Rank10's 0.4313 is the luckiest draw): W1 structure +0.005-0.012, W2 interpretive weave +0.006-0.016, W3 tables +0.001-0.004, W4 confession purge +0.001-0.003, W5 4IR framing +0.003-0.008. Bundle point estimate +0.02 to +0.04 overall → ~0.44-0.46. This DOES NOT reach 0.5265 — it falls ~0.07-0.09 short, and saying so is part of the plan. The residual lives in three places wave 1 deliberately does not touch: (a) comprehensiveness 'Scope of Industry-Specific Analysis' (w=0.25 of 0.29) — we have ZERO industry-organized content, and fixing it means regrouping evidence into a sectoral section at the outline seam, a compose-level change; (b) IF 'Exclusive Citation of High-Quality Journal Articles' (w=0.15 of 0.25) — post-ArticleCleaner the ONLY scoring path is venue names written into running prose, deterministically checkable against row metadata; (c) the deep one: the reference converts evidence into an ARGUMENT — a wave 2 would have to rebuild composition around comparison-matrix bundles (normalize by technology/unit-of-analysis/outcome/horizon, write from bundles not source cards) so synthesis is the unit of generation, not a post-hoc weave. Even a successful wave 2 honestly projects ~0.47-0.50; the last ~0.03 to ADORE's 0.5265 (a 100-task mean vs our n=1 task, so the comparison itself is soft) has no evidenced lever tonight. If wave 1's paired delta comes back <+0.008, even that ladder is dead and the honest report is 'task-72 RACE is insensitive to everything we can faithfully change.'
result.kill_rule: PRE-REGISTERED BEFORE ANY SCORING, in order: (0) INSTRUMENT GATE — W0's k=5 identical-artifact rescore first; if per-call SD > 0.020, STOP all scoring-driven work (no lever we own is resolvable) and fix the instrument before touching code. (1) MECHANISM GATE — no judge call is spent until every W1-W5 deterministic test is green on the CLEANED artifact and I have read 100% of the enumerated class-I sentences; the four skeptic attack cases must all DROP. (2) SHIP GATE — the bundle ships iff 5x-paired mean delta vs the pinned Rank10 baseline ≥ +0.010 (SE ~0.008). (3) DEATH GATE — if paired delta < +0.008, the structure+prose-mode thesis is DEAD ON THIS TASK: no variant iteration, no wave 2 built on it, write the negative result and escalate to the operator with the two remaining options (argument-first composer rebuild, or accept ~0.43 and redirect effort to the 100-task generalization where task-72 overfitting is worthless anyway). (4) FAITHFULNESS TRUMP — if ANY shipped sentence is found (regression test, audit read, or spot check) to carry a fabricated fact, number, or attribution, class I is stripped permanently regardless of score and the arm re-runs structure-only; the moat is not for sale. (5) SUNK-COST BOUND — wave 1 is ≤1 build-day and judge-calls-only (zero 65-minute composes; the entire arm runs on the banked outputs/rank10_sections_compose artifact); if the build itself exceeds that, the complexity is the warning and we stop and reassess. C1 and C2 remain dropped permanently unless someone produces code, not prose, that closes every enumerated hole.
